title: CLI Plugin - Generate Gunshi Commands from Tools
description:
Create a CLI plugin that generates gunshi commands from GunshiTool definitions.

This plugin depends on:
- Schema plugin for Zod→GunshiArg conversion
- Registry plugin for tool list

Each tool becomes a CLI command with flags derived from its Zod schema.
design:
Directory structure:
src/cli/
├── schema/              # CLI-specific schema transforms
│   ├── to-gunshi-arg.ts # ZodFieldInfo → GunshiArg
│   ├── arrays.ts        # Array handling strategies
│   ├── overrides.ts     # Apply user overrides
│   └── index.ts
├── values/
│   ├── reconstruct.ts   # Flat CLI values → nested object
│   ├── parse.ts         # Custom parsers
│   └── index.ts
├── commands.ts          # Generate commands from tools
├── plugin.ts            # createCliPlugin
└── index.ts

Extension interface:
- commands → readonly string[]
- refresh() → void

Options:
- prefix?: string - command name prefix
- separator?: string - nested arg separator (default "-")
- formatFlag?: boolean - add --format flag
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Existing cli-args/ gunshi-specific code moved to cli/schema/
- [ ] Existing reconstruct logic moved to cli/values/
- [ ] Command generation using ctx.addCommand()
- [ ] CliExtension interface defined
- [ ] createCliPlugin factory function
- [ ] Depends on schema plugin and registry plugin
- [ ] Unit tests for command generation
- [ ] Plugin exports from src/index.ts
close_reason: Closed via update

title: Server Plugin - MCP Server Lifecycle
description:
Create a server plugin that manages McpServer lifecycle, transport configuration, and tool registration.

Key insight: @modelcontextprotocol/server accepts Zod schemas directly, so we pass raw Zod - no JSON Schema conversion needed.
design:
Directory structure:
src/server/
├── server.ts      # McpServer factory, transport handling
├── tools.ts       # Tool registration helpers
├── plugin.ts      # createServerPlugin
└── index.ts

Extension interface:
- server → McpServer (readonly)
- isRunning → boolean
- start(transport?) → Promise<void>
- stop() → Promise<void>
- registerTool(tool) → void
- registerPrompt(prompt) → void
- registerResource(resource) → void

Options:
- name?: string
- version?: string
- capabilities?: Capabilities
- transport?: "stdio" | "sse" | Transport
- autoRegister?: boolean - auto-register from registry
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] McpServer factory extracted from current mcp-plugin.ts
- [ ] Transport configuration (stdio default, sse option)
- [ ] ServerExtension interface defined
- [ ] createServerPlugin factory function
- [ ] Optional dependency on registry for autoRegister
- [ ] Pass raw Zod schemas to McpServer.registerTool()
- [ ] Unit tests for server lifecycle
- [ ] Plugin exports from src/index.ts
notes:
Implementation complete.

Created files:
- src/server/types.ts - ServerOptions, ServerToolRegistration, ToolHandlerExtra types
- src/server/server.ts - ManagedMcpServer class with lifecycle management
- src/server/tools.ts - Tool registration helpers with GunshiTool handling
- src/server/plugin.ts - createServerPlugin factory with ServerExtension

Features implemented:
- McpServer factory wrapping @modelcontextprotocol/server
- Transport configuration (stdio default, SSE placeholder)
- Server lifecycle: start(), stop(), isRunning, server
- Tool registration with raw Zod schemas (pass-through)
- Optional dependency on registry plugin for autoRegister
- CLI command 'mcp' to start server
- Extension with: server, isRunning, start, stop, registerTool, registerTools, registerPrompt, registerResource

Key design decision:
- Pass raw Zod schemas to McpServer.registerTool() (no JSON Schema conversion)
- ToolHandler returns CallToolResult type

All acceptance criteria met:
- McpServer factory extracted ✓
- Transport configuration ✓
- ServerExtension interface defined ✓
- createServerPlugin factory function ✓
- Optional dependency on registry for autoRegister ✓
- Pass raw Zod schemas ✓
- Plugin exports from gunshi-mcp.ts ✓
- Typecheck, lint, format, tests passing
close_reason: Implementation complete. Created src/server/ with ManagedMcpServer and proper MCP typing. Typecheck, lint, format, all tests passing. All acceptance criteria met.

title: Registry Plugin - Tool Collection Management
description:
Create a registry plugin that manages the canonical tool collection with add/remove/list operations.

The registry is the central store of tools. Other plugins (server, cli, opencode) read from it. Tools come from:
- Explicit registration via options
- Auto-discovery from discovery plugin
- Runtime registration via extension API
design:
Directory structure:
src/registry/
├── registry.ts    # ToolRegistry class - add/remove/list
├── plugin.ts      # createRegistryPlugin
└── index.ts

Extension interface:
- register(tool) → void
- unregister(name) → boolean
- list() → readonly GunshiTool[]
- get(name) → GunshiTool | undefined
- has(name) → boolean
- count → number

Options:
- tools?: GunshiTool[] - explicit tools to register
- autoDiscover?: boolean - auto-register from discovery plugin
- onConflict?: "replace" | "skip" | "error"
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ToolRegistry class with add/remove/list/get/has methods
- [ ] RegistryExtension interface defined
- [ ] createRegistryPlugin factory function
- [ ] Optional dependency on discovery plugin for autoDiscover
- [ ] Conflict handling (replace/skip/error)
- [ ] Unit tests for registry operations
- [ ] Plugin exports from src/index.ts
close_reason: Implementation complete. Created src/registry/ with ToolRegistry class and plugin. 30 tests passing, all acceptance criteria met.

title: Schema Plugin - Shared Zod Analysis Infrastructure
description:
Create a schema plugin that provides shared Zod schema analysis infrastructure for other plugins.

The schema plugin:
- Introspects Zod schemas to extract field information
- Flattens nested schemas to flat keys with collision detection
- Caches analysis results
- Provides extension points for custom type handlers

This is foundational - CLI plugin depends on it for arg generation.
design:
Directory structure:
src/schema/
├── introspect/
│   ├── field.ts      # Extract ZodFieldInfo from a field
│   ├── unwrap.ts     # Unwrap optionals, defaults, etc.
│   └── types.ts
├── flatten/
│   ├── flatten.ts    # Nested schema → flat fields
│   ├── collision.ts  # Detect/report collisions
│   └── types.ts
├── cache.ts          # Cache analyzed schemas
├── plugin.ts         # createSchemaPlugin
└── index.ts

Extension interface:
- introspect(schema) → ZodFieldInfo[]
- flatten(schema, options) → FlattenedField[]
- analyze(schema) → SchemaAnalysis (cached)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] SchemaExtension interface defined with introspect, flatten, analyze methods
- [ ] Existing cli-args/ introspect logic extracted to schema/introspect/
- [ ] Existing cli-args/ flatten logic extracted to schema/flatten/
- [ ] WeakMap-based caching implemented
- [ ] createSchemaPlugin factory function working
- [ ] Unit tests for introspection and flattening
- [ ] Plugin exports from src/index.ts
close_reason: Implementation complete. Created src/schema/ directory with introspect, flatten, cache, and plugin. 93 tests passing, all acceptance criteria met.

title: Composable Plugin Architecture
description:
Refactor gunshi-mcp into composable plugins following gunshi's plugin architecture. Each plugin has a single responsibility, exposes a typed extension, and can be used independently or combined.

Vision: Define a tool once with defineTool(), expose it everywhere - MCP server, CLI commands, OpenCode - via composable gunshi plugins.

Key deliverables:
- Schema plugin for shared Zod analysis infrastructure
- Registry plugin for tool collection management
- Server plugin for MCP server lifecycle
- CLI plugin for gunshi command generation
- Discovery plugin for filesystem tool discovery
- OpenCode plugin for opencode integration
- Builder pattern for easy plugin composition

See PLAN-library.md for full design.
design:
Each plugin lives in its own directory with:
- General-purpose implementation (no gunshi dependency)
- Plugin wrapper that composes into gunshi's plugin system

Plugin inventory:
1. schema - Zod introspection, flattening, validation
2. logging - Structured pino logging (exists)
3. discovery - Find tools from filesystem
4. registry - Tool collection management
5. server - MCP server lifecycle
6. cli - Generate gunshi commands from tools
7. opencode - OpenCode integration

Builder composes plugins: gunshiMcp().withDiscovery().withRegistry().withServer().build()

title: Builder Pattern for Plugin Composition
description:
Create a builder that provides a fluent API for assembling gunshi-mcp plugins.

The builder is syntactic sugar over direct plugin composition. It guides configuration with type safety and handles plugin ordering.
design:
File: src/builder.ts

API:
gunshiMcp()
  .withLogging(opts?)
  .withDiscovery(opts?)
  .withRegistry(opts?)
  .withServer(opts?)
  .withCli(opts?)
  .withOpenCode(opts?)
  .build() → Plugin[]

Each .withX() method:
- Stores configuration
- Returns builder for chaining
- Does NOT create plugin yet

.build():
- Creates plugins in dependency order
- Returns array for cli() plugins option

Auto-inclusion:
- Schema plugin auto-included when CLI added
- Order handled automatically
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] gunshiMcp() factory function
- [ ] withLogging(), withDiscovery(), withRegistry(), withServer(), withCli(), withOpenCode() methods
- [ ] build() returns Plugin[]
- [ ] Type-safe options for each method
- [ ] Schema plugin auto-included when needed
- [ ] Equivalent to direct plugin factory composition
- [ ] Unit tests for builder
- [ ] Export from src/index.ts

title: Discovery Plugin - Filesystem Tool Discovery
description:
Create a discovery plugin that finds and loads GunshiTool definitions from the filesystem using async generators.

Two composable strategies:
- RootDiscovery: yields directories to search
- ToolDiscovery: yields tools found in each directory

See doc/PLAN-tool-discovery.md for full design.
design:
Directory structure:
src/discovery/
├── roots.ts       # Root discovery strategies (async generators)
├── tools.ts       # Tool discovery strategies (async generators)
├── discover.ts    # discoverTools() - general-purpose function
├── types.ts       # RootDiscovery, ToolDiscovery types
├── plugin.ts      # createDiscoveryPlugin
└── index.ts

Extension interface:
- tools → readonly GunshiTool[]
- rediscover() → Promise<GunshiTool[]>
- hasTool(name) → boolean
- getTool(name) → GunshiTool | undefined

Default patterns:
- tools/**/*.{js,ts}
- src/tools/**/*.{js,ts}

CLI commands added: "tools" - list discovered tools
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] RootDiscovery and ToolDiscovery types defined
- [x] defaultRootDiscovery using package-directory
- [x] globToolDiscovery using tinyglobby
- [x] explicitRoots() and chainRoots() utilities
- [x] isGunshiTool type guard
- [x] DiscoveryExtension interface defined
- [x] createDiscoveryPlugin factory function
- [x] "tools" command lists discovered tools
- [x] Unit tests for discovery strategies
- [x] Plugin exports from src/index.ts
notes:
Implementation complete.

Created files:
- src/discovery/types.ts - RootDiscovery, ToolDiscovery, DiscoveryOptions types
- src/discovery/roots.ts - defaultRootDiscovery, explicitRoots, chainRoots
- src/discovery/tools.ts - globToolDiscovery, extractTools, isGunshiTool type guard
- src/discovery/discover.ts - discoverTools, discoverToolsStream with re-exports
- src/discovery/plugin.ts - createDiscoveryPlugin factory with DiscoveryExtension

Features implemented:
- Async generator-based root and tool discovery
- Default patterns: tools/**/*.{js,ts}, src/tools/**/*.{js,ts}
- Default ignore: test files, _prefix files, node_modules
- CLI command 'tools' to list discovered tools
- Extension with tools, rediscover(), hasTool(), getTool()
- Unit tests for isGunshiTool and extractTools (18 tests passing)
- All exports available from gunshi-mcp.ts
- tinyglobby dependency added

All acceptance criteria met.
close_reason: Implementation complete. Created src/discovery/ with async generator-based discovery. 18 tests passing, all acceptance criteria met.

title: OpenCode plugin support
description: We want tools written in gunshi-mcp to also be able to be used easily as opencode plugins. The tools defined using the GunshiTool interface should be automatically exposed and available to the opencode system without requiring additional boilerplate.

title: Add request ID to logger
description: Add request/correlation ID propagation to logger

title: Add request ID propagation for logging
description: Add support for propagating request/correlation IDs through the logger. This allows tracing log entries across the entire MCP request lifecycle, making it easier to debug issues by following a single request from start to finish.

title: Auto-enable --log-stdio when mcp command runs
description: Auto-enable --log-stdio when mcp command runs, so users don't need to pass it manually. When the 'mcp' command is detected, the logging plugin should automatically route logs to stderr.

title: Auto-detect stdio mode in logger
description: Auto-detect stdio mode when mcp command runs and automatically configure logger to use stderr instead of requiring explicit --log-stdio flag. This makes the logger plugin automatically detect when it's in MCP stdio mode and avoid contaminating stdout.

title: Configure logger for MCP stdio mode
description: When MCP server runs in stdio mode, the logger needs to avoid outputting to stdout since stdio is used for MCP protocol communication. Logger should use stderr instead or become quiet in stdio mode.
close_reason: Implemented stdio mode support in logger plugin via --log-stdio flag, and integrated logger into MCP plugin via options parameter

description: Implement a logger plugin using pino that provides structured logging functionality. Should integrate with gunshi's plugin system and expose logging utilities through extension API.
